Technology.thesis: The AI capex cycle continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for chips, equipment, and related infrastructure. Software and services are also benefiting from AI integration and adoption, though some areas face scrutiny over revenue sustainability.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging equipment — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: While e-commerce and specialty retail show some resilience, broader consumer spending is under pressure from inflation. Residential construction faces headwinds, and auto manufacturers are navigating evolving market dynamics.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — signals persistent inflation, further squeezing consumer discretionary budgets
Consumer Confidence Index drops below 95 — indicates a significant decline in household optimism
Mortgage rates break above 8% — further dampens residential construction activity

Communication Services.thesis: While digital content and advertising remain strong, some companies face scrutiny over AI model guardrails and competitive pressures. The sector is adapting to evolving consumer preferences and technological shifts.
watchFor: Major social media platform reports significant user decline
New regulations on AI content generation or data usage
Economic slowdown impacts advertising budgets

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense is seeing strong backlogs and contract wins, while Integrated Freight & Logistics is navigating potential buyouts and tariff impacts. Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery faces a split market with construction booming but agriculture struggling.
watchFor: Major geopolitical de-escalation — reduces defense spending impetus
Global trade tensions escalate — negatively impacts freight volumes
Significant downturn in agricultural commodity prices — further pressures farm equipment sales

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant growth and investor interest, with regulatory approvals and pipeline advancements fueling the narrative. Healthcare plans maintain stability, while medical devices are seeing mixed signals.
watchFor: Unexpected safety concerns or side effects for GLP-1 drugs emerge
Major competitor launches a highly effective, cheaper GLP-1 alternative
New regulations significantly impact drug pricing or reimbursement

Financials.thesis: Major banks are clearing regulatory hurdles and showing strong year-to-date gains, while credit services and asset management firms are also demonstrating momentum and strategic growth.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed — compresses net interest margins for banks
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
New, stringent regulatory requirements for financial institutions

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Discount stores continue to demonstrate resilience amidst inflationary pressures, but broader consumer spending remains a concern for some segments. Household and personal products are showing momentum.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — further erodes consumer purchasing power
Significant drop in consumer confidence
Unexpected rise in unemployment

Utilities.thesis: Utilities are benefiting from the surge in AI data center demand, securing long-term power deals and demonstrating resilience as a recession-proof sector.
watchFor: Significant slowdown in AI data center buildout
New regulations capping energy prices for industrial users
Unexpected increase in fuel costs for power generation

Basic Materials.thesis: The sector is navigating geopolitical tensions around critical minerals and broader market dynamics, with mixed signals for individual companies.
watchFor: Escalation of trade wars impacting raw material supply
Significant downturn in global industrial production
New discoveries of rare earth elements outside of current dominant producers

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is rising due to easing geopolitical tensions and a weakening dollar, while silver's outlook is mixed after a recent plunge, awaiting clearer catalysts.
watchFor: Stronger-than-expected inflation data — could boost gold as a hedge
Significant strengthening of the US dollar — pressures gold prices
Resolution of major geopolitical conflicts — reduces safe-haven demand
